assumptions: The question concerns IFRS 9 hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend receivable creates a foreign-currency exposure within the group.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability and separately addresses intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model and discusses forecast intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context and IFRIC 16 identify the hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
